MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-06-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Tina Yim of IMAI, TADLOCK, KEENEY & CORDERY, LLP (415-675-7000 x4050), appearing via CourtCall for defendant and moving party Monterey Mechanical Co.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing via CourtCall for plaintiffs and opposing parties James Carmichael and Brenda Geisinger, as Successor-In-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Jerry Lynne Carmichael, Deceased.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Monterey Mechanical Co.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to identify the grounds for the motion in its notice and separate statement. (Code Civ. Proc. 1010; CRC rules 3.1110(a) and 3.1350(d).) Notwithstanding the defect, Defendant has not shown that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that James Carmichael, and thereby decedent, was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Defendant failed to address James Carmichael's interrogatory responses identifying numerous co-worker witnesses including Pat Farley, who was also identified as a person with knowledge. (Yim Declaration, Ex. E at 4:3-11; 8:9.) Defendant also failed to address James Carmichael's deposition testimony identifying Carl Grandin. (Yim Declaration, Ex. H at 658:20-659:4; 663:18-24; 675:7-10.) Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199, erin.robinson16@gmail.com (Tel: 925-785-7368) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Jul-23-2021 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Aug-06-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Shaghig D. Agopian of BUTY & CURLIANO (510-267-3000), appearing via CourtCall for defendant and moving party Petrochem Insulation Inc.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing via CourtCall for plaintiffs and opposing parties James Carmichael and Brenda Geisinger, as Successor-In-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Jerry Lynne Carmichael, Deceased.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling amended as follows: Defendant Petrochemical Insulation Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant failed to timely lodge courtesy copies of the moving papers with the Court. (Local Rule 2.7B.) Notwithstanding the defect, Defendant has not shown that Plaintiffs do not possess, and cannot reasonably obtain, needed evidence that James Carmichael, and thereby decedent, was exposed to asbestos containing products attributable to Defendant. (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 855.) Defendant failed to attach Plaintiffs Brenda Geisinger and James Carmichael's complete responses to Defendant's special interrogatories nos. 1-3 and 5, as well as responses to interrogatories nos. 6-28. (Ohlsen Declaration, Exs. C and D.) Defendant's fact no. 9 is misleading as James Carmichael testified that Pat Farley was not a Shell employee; he did not testify that Mr. Farley did not work at Shell Oil. (Ohlsen Declaration, Ex. E at 569:1-10.) Further, there is no deposition testimony that Mr. Carmichael took off all his work clothing including his underwear or that he never brought any of his garments home. (Ohlsen Declaration, Ex. E at 579:2-7.) There is no evidence that showering removed all traces of asbestos from Mr. Carmichael's body. The Court views the evidence and inference "in light most favorable to the opposing party." (Aguilar v. Atl. Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal. 4th 826, 843.)

Defenant's separate statement failed to identify the issue of duty. (CRC rules 3.1350(d)(1)(A) and (1)(B).) Even so, Defendant has not met its burden of showing that an exception to the general duty of care exists for this particular defendant. Kesner v. Superior Ct. (2016) 1 Cal. 5th 1132 was limited to the issue of whether employers or landowners have a duty of care to prevent secondary exposure to asbestos. Defendant failed to show a policy exception exists based on an analysis of the Rowland factors. (Id. at 1143.) Order signed in chambers. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199, erin.robinson16@gmail.com (Tel: 925-785-7368) (503/CML)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Aug-06-2021 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Emily D. Bergstrom of BHC Law Group, LLP (510-658-3600), appearing via CourtCall for defendant and moving party Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.
Andrew Chew of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing via CourtCall for plaintiffs and opposing parties James Carmichael and Brenda Geisinger, as Successor-In-Interest to and as Wrongful Death Heir of Jerry Lynne Carmichael, Deceased.

RULING:
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling amended as follows: Defendant Dillingham Construction N.A., Inc.'s Motion for Summary Judgment is DENIED. Defendant has not met its burden of showing that an exception to the general duty of care exists for this particular defendant. Kesner v. Superior Ct. (2016) 1 Cal. 5th 1132 was limited to the issue of whether employers or landowners have a duty of care to prevent secondary exposure to asbestos. Defendant failed to show a policy exception exists based on an analysis of the Rowland factors. (Id. at 1143.) There is insufficient evidence as to who controlled how defendant conducted its activities at Shell. Defendant failed to show that Mr. Carmichael's testimony that he oversaw Defendant's work on heat exchangers extended to oversight over all of Defendant's maintenance work. (Bergstrom Declaration, Ex. F at 1131:1-20.) Sabetian v. Exxon Mobil Corp. (2020) 57 Cal. App. 5th 1054 is distinguishable as that case did not involve take home exposure. Order signed in chambers. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Reporter: Erin F. Robinson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 12199, erin.robinson16@gmail.com (Tel: 925-785-7368) (503/CML)

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiff James Carmichael's Motion for order granting preference in setting case for trial and extending discovery cutoff is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(e). Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is August 5, 2021. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is July 30, 2021. Expert discovery cut-off date August 6, 2021.
